free_text: Measurement and evaluation of the excitation functions
for alpha particle induced nuclear reactions on niobium
Inst. of Nucl. Res. Debrecen, Hungary
High purity metallic foils (>99.9%) from Goodfellow.
12.5 micron Ti and 8 micron Cu monitor foils.
used. Stacks contained Nb, Ti and Cu foils were
irradiated at 20, 28 and 43 MeV for 0.5h with 200 nA.
Beam current was measured in Faraday-Cup and also
reactions. The whole excitation function was used for
monitoring from bombarding energy to threshold.
Decay data were taken from R.B.Firestone, V.S.Shirley
[Ed], Table of Isotopes, Wiley Interscience, New York,
and target foils were placed in the same stack. This
way the whole excitation function of the monitor
reaction was remeasured and compared with the
recommended values. It made possible simultaneous
determination of the bombarding energy and beam
intensity. Also in to check the energy degradation
The energy uncertainty was estimated to be 0.2
MeV for the primary beam and 2 MeV after 20 MeV
The energy attenuation was calculated by
using the polynomial approximation of Andersen and
Ziegler along the stacks. Energy staggling was
